api/System.Collections.Generic.Peekable-.title: Class Peekable<T> | DeviceOfHermes
api/System.Collections.Generic.Peekable-.summary: Class Peekable<T> Namespace System.Collections.Generic Assembly DeviceOfHermes.dll Provides a wrapper over an enumerator that supports peeking the next element without consuming it. public class Peekable<T> Type Parameters T Inheritance object Peekable<T> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ToString() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods Extension.Inspect<T>(T?, Action<T>) Examples var source = new List<int> { 1, 2, 3 }; var it = new Peekable<int>(source); if (it.Peek(out var next)) { Console.WriteLine(next); // 1 (not consumed yet) } it.MoveNext(out var current); Console.WriteLine(current); // 1 Remarks This class wraps an IEnumerator and allows inspecting the next element using Peek(out T?) without advancing the sequence. When Peek(out T?) is called, the value is cached internally. The subsequent call to MoveNext(out T?) will return the same value. Constructors Peekable(IEnumerable) Initializes a new instance of the Peekable<T> class from the specified enumerable source. public Peekable(IEnumerable enumrable) Parameters enumrable IEnumerable The source enumerable. Methods MoveNext(out T?) Advances to the next element and returns it. public bool MoveNext(out T? current) Parameters current T When this method returns, contains the current element if available; otherwise, the default value. Returns bool true if the next element exists; otherwise, false. Remarks If Peek(out T?) was previously called, this method returns the cached value and clears the internal state. Peek(out T?) Retrieves the next element without consuming it. public bool Peek(out T? peeked) Parameters peeked T When this method returns, contains the next element if available; otherwise, the default value. Returns bool true if the next element exists; otherwise, false. Remarks This method advances the underlying enumerator only once and caches the value internally so it can be returned again by MoveNext(out T?).

api/System.Collections.Generic.Walkdir.h.summary: Class Walkdir Namespace System.Collections.Generic Assembly DeviceOfHermes.dll Provides utilities for recursively walking directories. public static class Walkdir Inheritance object Walkdir Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ToString() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Examples var files = Walkdir.GetFilesRecursive(path); foreach (var file in files) { Console.WriteLine(file); } Remarks This class performs a depth-first traversal of directories and collects file paths. It keeps track of visited directories to prevent infinite loops caused by symbolic links or junctions. Invalid paths or non-directory inputs are silently ignored. Methods GetFilesRecursive(string) Recursively retrieves all file paths under the specified directory. public static List<string> GetFilesRecursive(string path) Parameters path string The root directory to traverse. Returns List<string> A list of file paths found under the specified directory. Returns an empty list if the path does not exist or is not a directory. Remarks This method performs a depth-first traversal. Directories that have already been visited are skipped to prevent infinite recursion. No exceptions are thrown for invalid paths; such inputs are ignored.
